signals.title: Sam Altman didn’t need another lawsuit
signals.desc: OpenAI has spent the better part of the year involved in lawsuit after lawsuit, including one from the world's richest man. But last Friday, the company was hit with one of the highest-profile legal actions yet - from Apple. OpenAI's expensive hardware bet is what's on the line. Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI, filed in Northern […]

signals.title: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
signals.desc: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality: most deployed “agents” are still chatbot wrappers, the control plane enterprises expect is deliberately hybrid to avoid lo

signals.title: Introducing the Codex Micro
signals.desc: See how the Codex Micro, a customizable keyboard built with Work Louder for Codex, helps you move through a build without leaving your flow. In this demo, GPT-5.6 Sol creates a word game while the Micro handles voice dictation, task switching, reasoning controls, permission requests, and follow-up prompts. Get yours: https://openai.com/supply/ Chapters: 00:00 Introducing the Codex Micro 00:24 Voic

signals.title: Thinking Machines Lab Drops Its First Model
signals.desc: Inkling, a 975-billion-parameter open source model, was trained to understand video and audio. It could help Thinking Machines establish itself among competitors like Anthropic and OpenAI.

signals.title: OpenAI researcher Miles Wang in talks to launch AI drug discovery startup valued at $2B
signals.desc: The funding discussions point to investor interest in applying AI to make breakthroughs in life sciences.

signals.title: Inside Ode with Anthropic, the startup betting AI services are the future of enterprise
signals.desc: Can a handful of engineers really do the work of an army of consultants? That’s the bet behind Ode with Anthropic — the joint venture dedicated to embedding forward-deployed engineers in enterprise firms, backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs and others. On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Rebecca Bellan sits down with Ode’s leaders Chris Taylor and Eddie S

signals.title: Anthropic, Blackstone bet the next trillion-dollar AI business is implementation, not just models
signals.desc: Anthropic-backed Ode launches as AI labs bet that embedding forward-deployed engineers inside enterprises is the key to accelerating enterprise AI adoption.

signals.title: Improved Intelligence with GPT-Live
signals.desc: A closer look at improved intelligence in GPT-Live: the model can keep a conversation going while helping with multiple tasks at once, like checking flights, pulling up local weather, and shaping an itinerary in real time. More about our next generation voice model here: https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-live/

signals.title: Apple Intelligence approved for launch in China with Alibaba’s Qwen AI
signals.desc: The deal, which was rumored to be in the works last year, marks an important step for Apple's AI ambitions in a key market.

signals.title: Introducing Claude for Teachers
Build and publish web apps directly in ChatGPT
signals.desc: See how to build a working web app directly in ChatGPT. Describe what you want, try the first version, ask for changes in plain language, and publish it when it’s ready—with hosting and storage built in. Learn more: https://learn.chatgpt.com/ Chapters 00:00 Introducing Sites 00:21 Building a launch site 00:41 Refining with feedback 00:55 Annotating and publishing 01:09 Working with Build Web Apps

signals.title: Amid hardware legal battle, OpenAI releases a $230 keyboard for Codex
signals.desc: OpenAI, which is in the middle of a legal battle with Apple over hardware trade theft allegations, just released a light-up keyboard designed to be paired with its agentic coding app.

signals.title: Building the infrastructure for the Intelligence Age in Michigan
signals.desc: OpenAI breaks ground on a 1GW data center project in Michigan as part of Stargate, building AI infrastructure to expand access, create jobs, and support communities.

signals.title: Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer
signals.desc: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. Last week the company released the latest version of its flagship LLM, GPT-5.6. OpenAI says that training it against GPT-Red made the model its most robust release yet. GPT-Red automates…

signals.title: OpenAI's first branded hardware is... a light-up keyboard?
signals.desc: The Codex Micro is designed to monitor multiple agentic threads at a glance.

signals.title: The US is advancing AI safety through state and federal action
signals.desc: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.

signals.title: Anthropic launches Claude Tag, a tool that works like a virtual employee within Slack
AI slop movies are the new direct-to-video cash grabs
signals.desc: This weekend, cinephiles across the world will march to their local theaters to feast their eyes on Christopher Nolan's new adaptation of The Odyssey. It's on track to rake in anywhere between $80-$100 million in just a few days. People are clearly excited to see how Nolan uses cutting-edge filmmaking tech to make the Homeric […]

signals.title: SpaceX falls to $135 IPO price ahead of Starship launch
signals.desc: The stock has steadily fallen from the euphoric post-IPO high, showing that markets may be sobering up to the promises CEO Elon Musk made before and after SpaceX went public.

signals.title: GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness
signals.desc: Explore GPT-Red, OpenAI’s automated red teaming system that uses self-play to improve AI safety, alignment, and prompt injection robustness.

signals.title: Bristol Myers to deploy Anthropic's Claude AI model to speed up drug discovery
Suno snatched millions of songs from YouTube, Genius, and Deezer

Given that Suno has avoided revealing what's in its training datasets and how they were acquired, this a rare […]

signals.title: Whatnot acquires Shaped to power real-time live shopping recommendations
signals.desc: Livestream shopping platform Whatnot has acquired AI startup Shaped, a machine learning company focused on real-time recommendations and search. The deal will bolster Whatnot’s personalization and discovery features as it expands into new product categories.

signals.title: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show
signals.desc: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Anthropic—currently the world’s most valuable AI company, with a nearly $1 trillion valuation—has a reputation for publishing strange and heady research. It’s looking into whether AI models can feel pain, for example,…

signals.title: SpaceXAI’s Grok programming tool was uploading its users’ entire codebase to cloud storage
signals.desc: SpaceXAI's Grok Build AI coding tool was spotted uploading users' entire codebases to Google Cloud before it was reported, and the company turned it off. The Register reports that Cereblab published findings on Monday showing how the Grok Build CLI was packaging and uploading entire code repositories, "including files it was told not to open […]

signals.title: China's Alibaba bans Anthropic AI for employees after 'distillation attack' accusation
Rime picks up $24M Series A to help enterprises field customer calls
signals.desc: Rime is handling over 100 million calls each month across multiple companies.

signals.title: Indian AI coding startup Emergent becomes a unicorn with $130M Series C
signals.desc: The startup has reached a $120 million annualized revenue run rate and more than 200,000 paying customers.

signals.title: How sales teams use ChatGPT Work
signals.desc: See how sales teams can use ChatGPT Work to create pipeline briefs, meeting prep packets, forecast reviews, account plans, and stalled-deal diagnoses from real work inputs.

signals.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
signals.desc: The device is weirdly described as involving "mechanical elements that can move on their own" and the Bloomberg report includes the detail that the device is designed to "feel like a companion and become a physical manifestation of OpenAI’s ChatGPT."

signals.title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
signals.desc: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. signals.title: Anthropic releases Claude Science, a product aimed at researchers, the pharma industry
OpenAI’s new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
signals.desc: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.

signals.title: Anthropic’s newest ad is creeping people out
signals.desc: Anthropic has consistently attempted to depict itself as the ethical foil to other AI companies. This latest marketing stunt — which leans into criticism of AI as a way to make Anthropic seem aware of the responsibility it carries — would appear to be more of the same.

signals.title: Anthropic pushes deeper into healthcare with Optum tie-up, UST integration
Empowering India’s next generation of innovators with ATL Saathi
signals.desc: Google and AIM launched ATL Saathi, a Gemini-powered AI tool empowering Indian educators in robotics labs.

signals.title: OpenAI may have made a fatal misstep in copyright fight with news orgs
signals.desc: OpenAI may be sanctioned for hiding, deleting ChatGPT logs in NYT copyright fight.

signals.title: My Ebike Delivery Went Missing. When I Tried to Recover It, I Ended Up in Chatbot Hell
signals.desc: Companies’ increasing reliance on AI chatbots isn’t making the customer service experience smarter. It’s just making it more infuriating.

signals.title: How Claude's values vary by model and language

signals.desc: Explore OpenAI’s Frontier Governance Framework and how our AI safety, security, and risk practices align with emerging EU and California regulations.
